---
title: "Final Project Figures"
format: pdf
---

Setup directory and data:

```{python}

import pandas as pd
import altair as alt
import geopandas as gpd
from pathlib import Path
import os

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent
SAVE_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'figures')

os.chdir(BASE_DIR)

full_data = pd.read_csv('data\derived_data\Full Data.csv')
full_data = full_data[full_data['Ownership'] != 'Total Covered']
```

Create a list of variable to loop over:

```{python}

vars = [
      'Gini (Standardized)',
      'Median House Price for Below-Median Income Families (Standardized)',
      'Poverty Rate (Standardized)', 
      'Murder and nonnegligent manslaughter_rate',
      'Income Segregation (Standardized)',
      'Location Affordability of Very Low-Income Individual',
      'School Expenditure per Student (Standardized)',
      'Labor Force Participation (Standardized)',
      'Migration Outlflow Rate (Standardized)',
      'Fraction Religious (Standardized)',
      'Fraction of Children with Single Mothers (Standardized)',
      'Violent crime_rate',
      'Property crime_rate',
      'Racial Segregation (Standardized)',
      'Number of Colleges per Capita (Standardized)',
      'High School Dropout Rate (Income adjusted) (Standardized)',
      'College Graduation Rate (Income Adjusted) (Standardized)',
      'Teenage (14-16) Labor Force Participation (Standardized)',
      'Median House Rent for Below-Median Income Families (Standardized)',
      'Unemployment Rate (Standardized)',
      'Fraction of Adults Married (Standardized)'
]

county_summary = full_data.groupby('County FIPS 2000').agg({
    'Local Wage Share': lambda x: 1 / (x**2).sum(),
    'Gini (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Median House Price for Below-Median Income Families (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Poverty Rate (Standardized)': 'first', 
    'Murder and nonnegligent manslaughter_rate': 'first',
    'Income Segregation (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Location Affordability of Very Low-Income Individual': 'first',
    'School Expenditure per Student (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Labor Force Participation (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Migration Outlflow Rate (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Fraction Religious (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Fraction of Children with Single Mothers (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Violent crime_rate': 'first',
    'Racial Segregation (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Number of Colleges per Capita (Standardized)': 'first',
    'High School Dropout Rate (Income adjusted) (Standardized)': 'first',
    'College Graduation Rate (Income Adjusted) (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Teenage (14-16) Labor Force Participation (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Median House Rent for Below-Median Income Families (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Unemployment Rate (Standardized)': 'first',
    'Fraction of Adults Married (Standardized)': 'first',
    'kr26_p25_coef': 'first',
    'Property crime_rate': 'first',
    'Household Income per Capita': 'first',
    'kr26_p75_coef': 'first',
    'Median House Price for Above-Median Income Families (Standardized)': 'first'
})

county_summary = county_summary.rename(columns={
    'Local Wage Share': 'Inverse HHI'
})

```

Now, calculate correlation of our variables with Inverse HHI
```{python}

outcome = 'Inverse HHI'
results = []

for var in vars:

    clean_data = county_summary[[var, outcome]].dropna()

    #correlation for easy interpretability (in range [-1, 1])
    correlation = clean_data[var].corr(clean_data[outcome])

    #run regression on outcome and HHI to get relationship, p-values
    from scipy import stats
    slope, intercept, r_value, p_value, std_err = stats.linregress(
        clean_data[var],
        clean_data[outcome]
          )

    n = len(clean_data)
    t_critical = stats.t.ppf(0.975, n-2)
    ci_lower = slope - (t_critical * std_err)
    ci_upper = slope + (t_critical * std_err)    

    mean_concentration = clean_data[outcome].mean()

    #get SD of given variable
    var_std = clean_data[var].std()

    #calculate effect of 1 SD increase on HHI, with 95% CIs
    effect_per_sd = slope * var_std
    effect_ci_lower = ci_lower * var_std
    effect_ci_upper = ci_upper * var_std

    #convert to percent
    pct_effect = (effect_per_sd / mean_concentration) * 100
    pct_ci_lower = (effect_ci_lower / mean_concentration) * 100
    pct_ci_upper = (effect_ci_upper / mean_concentration) * 100
    
    results.append({
              'Variable': var,
              'Effect_per_SD': effect_per_sd,
              'Pct_Change': pct_effect,
              'Correlation': correlation,
              'CI_lower': pct_ci_lower,
              'CI_upper': pct_ci_upper,
              'P_value': p_value,
              'Significant': p_value < 0.05
          })

results_df = pd.DataFrame(results)

```

Clean up results:

```{python}

#filter for statistically significant relationships
results_signif = results_df[results_df['Significant'] == True]

#clean up variable names for plotting
results_signif['Variable'] = results_signif['Variable'].str.replace(' (Standardized)', '', regex=False)
results_signif['Variable'] = results_signif['Variable'].str.replace('(Income adjusted)', '', regex=False)
results_signif['Variable'] = results_signif['Variable'].str.replace('Teenage (14-16) Labor Force Participation', 'Teen (14-16) LFP', regex=False)
results_signif['Variable'] = results_signif['Variable'].str.replace(' for Below-Median Income Families', ', Low-Income', regex=False)
results_signif['Variable'] = results_signif['Variable'].str.replace(' of Very Low-Income Individual', ', Very Low-Income', regex=False)

#convert correlation to string for plotting
results_signif['Correlation'] = results_signif['Correlation'].astype(str)

#sort by biggest change to avoid "sort=" arg in altair - it breaks text
results_signif = results_signif.sort_values('Pct_Change', ascending=False)
ordered_vars = results_signif['Variable'].tolist()

```

Plot as a points + error bars with correlation coefficients on a column to the right:

```{python}

#base chart - diamonds showing relationship
points = alt.Chart(results_signif).mark_point(
    color="#40248c",
    shape='diamond',
    size=150,
    opacity=1,
    filled=True
    ).encode(
    x=alt.X('Pct_Change:Q',
            title='Change in Wage Concentration (%)',
            scale=alt.Scale(domain=[-20, 35])),  
    y=alt.Y('Variable:O',
            scale=alt.Scale(domain=ordered_vars),
            title='Crime and Mobility Measures')
).properties(height=500, width=800,
             title={
        "text": "Association Between 1 SD Increase of Economic Measures with Wage Concentration",
        "subtitle": "With Associated Correlation Coefficients and Confidence Intervals",
        })

#confidence intervals
errorbars = alt.Chart(results_signif).mark_errorbar(
    ticks=True,
    size=10,
    color='black'
).encode(
    x=alt.X('CI_lower:Q', title=''),
    x2='CI_upper:Q',
    y=alt.Y('Variable:O',
    scale=alt.Scale(domain=ordered_vars))
)

#correlation coefficent to the right
corr_text = alt.Chart(results_signif).mark_text(
).encode(
    x=alt.value(770),
    y=alt.Y('Variable:O',
            scale=alt.Scale(domain=ordered_vars),
            title=None),
    text=alt.Text('Correlation:Q', format='.2f'))

#zero line to delineate positive and negative relationships
zero_line_x = alt.Chart(pd.DataFrame({'x': [0]})).mark_rule(
    strokeDash=[5, 5],
    color='black'
).encode(x='x:Q')

#put layers together
wage_chart = (errorbars + points + zero_line_x + corr_text).configure_view(clip=False)

#save to figures folder
wage_chart.save(os.path.join(SAVE_DIR, 'Wage Concentration Chart.png'), scale_factor=2)

```

Aggregate data for bar chart:

```{python}
county_summary['housing_quint_high'] = pd.qcut(county_summary['Median House Price for Above-Median Income Families (Standardized)'], q=5, labels = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

county_summary['housing_quint_low'] = pd.qcut(county_summary['Median House Price for Below-Median Income Families (Standardized)'], q=5, labels = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

high_df = county_summary.groupby('housing_quint_high').agg({'kr26_p75_coef':
  'mean'}).reset_index()

high_df['Income'] = '75th Percentile'

high_df.rename(columns={'housing_quint_high': 'quintile', 'kr26_p75_coef':
   'mobility'}, inplace=True)

low_df = county_summary.groupby('housing_quint_low').agg({'kr26_p25_coef':
   'mean'}).reset_index()
low_df['Income'] = '25th Percentile'
low_df.rename(columns={'housing_quint_low': 'quintile', 'kr26_p25_coef':
  'mobility'}, inplace=True)

combined_df = pd.concat([high_df, low_df])
```

Plot mobility by housing prices, shared x-axis for different income families:

```{python}
chart = alt.Chart(combined_df).mark_bar().encode(
      x=alt.X('quintile:O', title='Housing Price Quintile'),
      y=alt.Y('mobility:Q', title='Mean Mobility Coefficient (Gain in Percentile)'),
      color=alt.Color('Income:N', scale=alt.Scale(range=['#46327E', '#B63679']), title='Parent Income'),
      xOffset='Income:N'
  ).properties(
      width=400,
      height=300,
      title={
        'text':'Mean Mobility by Housing Prices, Below- and Above-Median Income Families',
        'subtitle': 'As Measured by Child Rank in National Income Distribution at Age 26 '
  })

zero_line_y = alt.Chart(pd.DataFrame({'y': [0]})).mark_rule(
    strokeDash=[5, 5],
    color='black'
).encode(y='y:Q')

final_bar = (chart + zero_line_y).configure_view(clip=False)

final_bar.save(os.path.join(SAVE_DIR, 'Mobility by Housing Chart.png'), scale_factor=2)
```
```{python}
# set up
import geopandas as gpd
from pathlib import Path
import os
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib as mpl
import altair as alt

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent.parent
BASE_DIR
SAVE_DIR = os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'figures')
os.chdir(BASE_DIR)

data_w_geo= gpd.read_file(os.path.join("data\derived_data\Full Data with Geography.gpkg"))

county_map = gpd.read_file(os.path.join('data\Shapefiles\County\co99_d00.shp'))

#drop Alaska and Hawaii
county_map = county_map[county_map['STATE'] != '02']
county_map = county_map[county_map['STATE'] != '15']

#want to collapse to single row per county
data_grouped_geo = data_w_geo[
    (data_w_geo["Ownership"] == "Private") &
    (data_w_geo["Industry"] == "Total, all industries")]

data_grouped_geo = data_grouped_geo[data_grouped_geo["state_name"]!="Hawaii"]

#want to winsorize property crime to display better
pctile95 = data_grouped_geo['Property crime_rate'].quantile(0.95)
data_grouped_geo["Property crime_rate_winsor"] = np.where(data_grouped_geo["Property crime_rate"] > pctile95, 
                                                     pctile95, 
                                                     data_grouped_geo["Property crime_rate"]) 
pctile95 = data_grouped_geo['Violent crime_rate'].quantile(0.95)
data_grouped_geo["Violent crime_rate_winsor"] = np.where(data_grouped_geo["Violent crime_rate"] > pctile95, 
                                                     pctile95, 
                                                     data_grouped_geo["Violent crime_rate"])                                                      
```


```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 8))

county_map.plot(
    color='lightgrey',   
    ax=ax)

data_grouped_geo.plot(
    column='Property crime_rate_winsor',              
    linewidth=0.2,           
    edgecolor='0.5',          
    legend=True,              
    ax=ax,
    zorder=2,
    cmap='magma_r'
    )

cbar_ax = fig.axes[-1]
cbar_ax.set_title('Property Crime Rate\n(per 100,000 residents)', fontsize=10)
ax.set_position([0.03, 0.08, 0.82, 0.85])
cbar_ax.set_position([0.87, 0.25, 0.02, 0.5])

ax.set_title('County-Level Property Crime Rates', fontsize=24)
ax.axis('off')  

ax.set_axis_off()
save_path = os.path.join(SAVE_DIR, 'property_crime.png')
plt.savefig(save_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
```


```{python}
# now for violent crime
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 8))

county_map.plot(
    color='lightgrey',   
    ax=ax)

data_grouped_geo.plot(
    column='Violent crime_rate_winsor',              
    linewidth=0.2,           
    edgecolor='0.5',          
    legend=True,              
    ax=ax,
    zorder=2,
    cmap='magma_r',
    )

cbar_ax = fig.axes[-1]
cbar_ax.set_title('Violent Crime Rate\n(per 100,000 residents)', fontsize=10)
ax.set_position([0.03, 0.08, 0.82, 0.85])
cbar_ax.set_position([0.87, 0.25, 0.02, 0.5])

ax.set_title('County-Level Violent Crime Rates', fontsize=24)
ax.axis('off')  

ax.set_axis_off()
save_path = os.path.join(SAVE_DIR, 'violent_crime.png')
plt.savefig(save_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

```


```{python}
# Now for mobility

fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 8))

county_map.plot(
    color='lightgrey',   
    ax=ax)

norm = mpl.colors.Normalize(vmin=-9, vmax=7)
data_grouped_geo.plot(
    column='kr26_p25_coef',              
    linewidth=0.2,           
    edgecolor='0.5',          
    legend=True,              
    ax=ax,
    zorder=2,
    cmap='magma_r',
    norm=norm
    )

cbar_ax = fig.axes[-1]
cbar_ax.set_title('Mobility Coefficient', fontsize=10)
ax.set_position([0.03, 0.08, 0.82, 0.85])
cbar_ax.set_position([0.87, 0.25, 0.02, 0.5])

ax.set_title('County-Level Mobility for 25th percentile', fontsize=24)
ax.axis('off')  

ax.set_axis_off()
save_path = os.path.join(SAVE_DIR, 'mobility_25.png')
plt.savefig(save_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(12, 8))

county_map.plot(
    color='lightgrey',   
    ax=ax)

data_grouped_geo.plot(
    column='kr26_p75_coef',              
    linewidth=0.2,           
    edgecolor='0.5',          
    legend=True,              
    ax=ax,
    zorder=2,
    cmap='magma_r',
    )

cbar_ax = fig.axes[-1]
cbar_ax.set_title('Mobility Coefficient', fontsize=10)
ax.set_position([0.03, 0.08, 0.82, 0.85])
cbar_ax.set_position([0.87, 0.25, 0.02, 0.5])

ax.set_title('County-Level Mobility for 75th percentile', fontsize=24)
ax.axis('off')  

ax.set_axis_off()
save_path = os.path.join(SAVE_DIR, 'mobility_75.png')
plt.savefig(save_path, dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
```
